X.T: Using Code Syntax Parsing for Generative AI

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Codeium has created and open-sourced a tool to query syntax tree built with Tree-sitter.

X.T: Be Careful Where you Send Your Code

ARTICLE.COMMENT: If you want to keep your code private, deploy your tools on premise. This is why Codeium proposes an Enterprise offering.

X.T: Why You Should Not Trust All the Numbers You See

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A critique of the metrics provided by Codeium’s competitors and how Codeium evaluates its product.

X.T: Codeium Brings VS Codium AI Powers

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Codeium plugins are now available in the OpenVSX registry.
Codeium adds a mechanism to explicitly ask to generate code.

X.T: Introducing Codeium Live: Free, Forever Up-to-Date In-Browser Chat

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A chat answering questions on recent versions of some libraries.

X.T: GitHub Copilot’s Security Filters Don’t Work

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Demonstrating that GitHub Copilot’s claims to filter out unsafe code are bullshit.
A description of how Codeium handles code licences: filter out code that is explicitly non-permissively licensed from training data, attribution of generated code…
A description on how Codeium expects to manage experimental features.

X.T: What We Think of 2023 Q4 AI News

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Some comments on GitHub Copilot announcements and its dependency to OpenAI, and further experimentation on the poor efficacy of CodeWhisperer.

X.T: The Effect of Generative AI on the Human-Tool Interface

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The vision of Codeium of how Generative AI helps the user and how this vision shapes the human-tool interface they implement.

X.T: Streamlining AI Code Chat with @ Mentions

ARTICLE.COMMENT: @ Mentions have been improved, but a real documentation of that feature would be helpful.
The title says it all.
Codeium models have been trained on MongoDB APIs and syntax.

X.T: Latency, the Ultimate Gen AI Constraint

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Some interesting optimisations implemented by Codeium to reduce latency.

X.T: How Generative AI is Accelerating Software Development w/ Codeium Co-Founder | Technovation 842

ARTICLE.COMMENT: An interesting interview of VarunMohan, the CEO of Codeium.

X.T: Remote Indexing and Multi-repository Context Awareness

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The importance of being able to use whole repositories to find the right context to answer a question or to generate autocompletion, and the new possibility to index these repositories.
The title says it all.
It is now possible, in VS Code, to pin a context so get more relevant autocompletions or chat answers.
A description of the enhancements in the last Codeium release.

A long description of Codeium’s strategy and new products: Cortex/Cascade performs tasks on whole projects by defining a plan and performing each step (all this being modifiable by a human), Forge assists code reviews, and Guidelines for customising Codeium’s tools (currently Forge) for a company specific needs.
Copy in one a language, paste in another one.
Codeium is still performing very well.
Codeium is introducing a paying tier for individual users.
A more powerful autocomplete that, as Cursor, can insert at other places than the cursor position.
A usual, Codeium’s discourse on metrics and analytics is much more evaluable than the bullshit of GitHub ou GitLab.
Codeium is victim of its success: the Pro Tier gets more expensive, but includes more actions.

X.T: Why Developers Trust Windsurf to Write 90% of Their Code (with Anthropic)

ARTICLE.COMMENT: An interesting presentation of Windsurf strategy with little marketing bullshit (especially compared to the current bullshit AI-related tsunami).

X.ST: Varun Mohan, CEO of Windsurf, shares how building an AI-native IDE is reshaping software development—from optimizing LLM latency to enabling non-engineers to ship code.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Some information about Windsurf, the company and the IDE, and some musing on the impact of AI on developers.
Windsurf stops its self-hosted offering and replaces it with new single-tenant hosted offering.

X.T: OpenAI’s Windsurf deal is off — and Windsurf’s CEO is going to Google
X.ST: Key researchers from the AI coding startup are also heading to Google.

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The title and subtitle say it all.
The official announcement.
Cognition is buying Windsurf‽⸘
The corresponding video.

X.T: Google, OpenAI, Chaos: Inside Windsurf's $3B Deal Implosion (72 hrs)

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The story of Windsurf and, in particular, the last weekend.

X.T: Introducing SWE-1.5: Our Fast Agent Model

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Some notes about Cognition’s new model.

X.T: Windsurf Arena Mode Leaderboard: The People Want Speed

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The first results from Windsurf’s new Arena Mode.
Codeium is proposing it owns IDE, yet another VS Code fork.

X.T: Codeium's Windsurf : This NEW & FULLY FREE AI Editor BEATS CURSOR!

ARTICLE.COMMENT: AICodeKing is discovering Codeium…

X.T: Windsurf Editor TUTORIAL // Better than Cursor? (yes)

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A presentation of Windsurf by someone who discovered it recently.

X.T: Windsurf (Upgraded) : This is The BEST AI Editor Right Now! (NEW Image Upload, Keybindings & More!)

ARTICLE.COMMENT: There is almost no new information in this video, except for the possibility to add images in the prompt.
Windsurf 1.1 adds the possibility of defining global and workspace rules, and to customise terminal commands not requiring approval.

X.T: Innovation, not Productivity: Why we Built Windsurf

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Codeium explains that AI code assistants are not only about developer productivity: more persons can produce applications while not being developers, people can work on code bases they do not master…
Web search, automated memories, terminal integration, and problem tab integration.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 2 Update: Web Search, Autogenerated Memories, and More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The corresponding video.

X.T: Turn Blog Post Tutorials into Full-Stack Applications Automatically - Windsurf Editor

ARTICLE.COMMENT:
A demo of generating code from a blog posthttps://khou22.com/blog/building-a-waitlist-in-2023
enHTML.

X.T: Save Time and Flow Action Credits with Cascade Autogenerated Memories - Windsurf Editor

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Cascade is now able to decide by itself to add data in its memory. But it is unclear when it does so. I guess you will have some feelings about its behaviour by using it.

X.T: Spend Less Time Approving with Automated Command - Windsurf Editor

ARTICLE.COMMENT: This demo of automated command, where you can allow Cascade to execute safe commands without asking for approval, is unclear.
The title says it all.

X.T: DeepSeek R1 and OpenAI o3-mini now available in Windsurf

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The title says it all.
Codeium now provides a beta version of Windsurf.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 3 Updates: Tab to Jump, MCP, Custom App Icons, Turbo Mode & More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The title says it all.
The same.

X.T: A Beginner's Guide to using MCP in Windsurf!

ARTICLE.COMMENT: An explanation on how to use MCP.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 4 Updates: Preview, Tab to Import, Suggested Actions & More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Preview, tab-to-import, suggested actions, auto-fixing lint violations, easier MCP configuration, referral program, drag ’n drop files, improved tool support in Sonnet 3.7…
The same.

X.T: Windsurf Previews: Streamline UI Iteration & Debugging

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Preview a Web App in Windsurf so an HTML element can be added to the context and Windsurf can access the error messages in the console to debug the App.
Codeium merges the tab features (complete, super-complete, jump, import) into a single feature having a larger context: text editor, terminal, dialog, and clipboard.
The same.
One-click deployment on Netlify, commit message generation, easier navigation of the chat history, improved long chats, more information pushed in the Tab context…

X.T: Windsurf Wave 6 Update: Deploy, Commit Message Generation, Jupyter Notebook Support And More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The same.
Cascade is now integrated in JetBrains IDEs.
The same.
Windsurf integrates GPT 4.1 and gives it for free for one week.

X.T: Day 1 of Windsurf Wave 8: Windsurf Reviews, Knowledge Base, Conversation Sharing & More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Code reviews, conversation sharing, interrogating Google Docs, deploying in Netlify team, and analytics.

X.T: Day 2 of Windsurf Wave 8: Custom Workflows, Multiple Cascades, File-Based Rules & More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Custom workflows, granular rules files, simultaneous cascades, and improved MCP support.

X.T: Day 3 of Windsurf Wave 8: JetBrains & UX Updates

ARTICLE.COMMENT: UX improvements: model selector, edit proposed terminal command, opening conversations switches to the corresponding workspace, improved commit messages…

X.T: Windsurf Wave 9: Introducing SWE-1, Our Frontier Models

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Windsurf proposes a new family of models for handling the whole software engineering process.
More information about the models and Windsurf’s strategy.

X.T: An Inflection Point for U.S. Government

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Windsurf explains what they are the best option for federal agencies and regulated industries.
Windsurf has some problems with Anthropic, I guess this is related to being bought by OpenAI.
Cascade is now able to define and to maintain a plan of the tasks to be done.
A much more detailed and informative description of Windsurf’s vision on supporting long-term plan.
Windsurf now provides a Browser in order to provide context to Cascade more easily.
Windsurf explains why they now deliver their own Chrome fork.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 10 - Day 3: Enterprise + UX Updates

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Update of Percentage of Code Written, European cluster, coloured code suggestion, access to past conversation from the current one, file-based icons, interaction with suggested command terminals, and removal of legacy mode.
The same.
Some thoughts about the advantages and caveats of the PCW metric.

X.T: How to Use MCP Servers in Windsurf AI (Becoming 10x Developer)
X.ST: Unlock MCP servers in Windsurf AI with this 2025 tutorial—step-by-step setup, real-world uses, and pro tips for seamless coding flow!

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A very basic introduction on using MCP in Windsurf, without a single advice on security.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 11: Voice, Deeper Browser Integrations, JetBrains Improvements and More

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Voice support, referencing past conversations and Browser tabs, planning mode is now the default, conversations checkpoints…
The same.

X.T: OpenAI's GPT-5 out now on Windsurf!

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Windsurf gets GPT-5 support as soon as this one is released. OpenAI explains their goals were personality, intelligence, and steerability. They give examples of how steerable it is.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 12: Devin features in Windsurf!

ARTICLE.COMMENT: DeepWiki, Vibe and Replace, automated use of planning mode, better use of models having long context, improved Tab, improved UI, bug fixes, and dev container support.

X.T: Windsurf Wave 12: Devin features in Windsurf

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The same.
You can now queue prompts while Windsurf is processing the current one.
The title says it all.

X.T: Introducing SWE-grep and SWE-grep-mini: RL for Multi-Turn, Fast Context Retrieval

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A description, with some technical details of the training, of SWE-grep and SWE-grep-mini, two models focused on finding the relevant context as fast as possible in the code base.

X.T: Windsurf Codemaps: Understand Code, Before You Vibe It

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Cognition introduces Codemaps, but this article gives little information about its use, it is mostly marketing speech.

X.ST: Wave 13: Shipmas - Parallel Agents, Git Worktrees, and Near-Frontier Coding Performance For Free With SWE-1.5

ARTICLE.COMMENT: Cognition delivers at last a new wave. The subtitle says it all.

X.T: Wave 14: Arena Mode - May the Best Model Win

ARTICLE.COMMENT: A long description of the integration of an arena mechanism in Windsurf, which does not seem that interesting for me, and a short presentation of the plan mode, which is probably more impactful on how to use Windsurf.

X.T: Windsurf Tab v2: 25-75% more accepted code with Variable Aggression

ARTICLE.COMMENT: The guys from Cognition are badmouthing the former Windsurf team and explain that they improved Tab completion/navigation.
